rag_rules: Place input range assumptions in component type annexes using visible feature names and concrete bounds.
Use assign statements only for implementation-level output bindings with existing output features.
Generate syntactically valid AGREE contracts within annex agree blocks using valid AADL scope identifiers.
generic_rules: Ensure AGREE assumptions and guarantees reference only visible identifiers from the AADL model.
Declare helper variables with explicit types matching the expressions they represent.
Avoid encoding AADL properties as AGREE variables unless explicitly exposed as typed ports or constants.
Validate that Boolean expressions are only used when the port is a native AGREE bool type.
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: AGREE contracts must be enclosed in `annex agree {** ... **};` blocks.
Component type contracts use feature names directly; implementation contracts use subcomponent bindings.
Boolean helper declarations must match the type of the comparison expression.
anti_patterns: Do not place implementation-level guarantees in component type scopes.
Do not reference AADL property names like Period or Deadline as AGREE variables.
Do not convert Boolean domain values into integer comparisons unless numeric scalar representation is provided.
irrelevant_topics: Model fusion and owner/scope preservation rules
